---
title: "Extracellular Electrophysiology"
subtitle: "Session 1"
author: "OSSS Ephys Course 2026"
pagetitle: "Extracellular Electrophysiology"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    css: custom.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <meta name="referrer" content="strict-origin-when-cross-origin">
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    incremental: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    footer: "OSSS Ephys Course 2026"
---

## Today

::: {.incremental}
1. Introduction to extracellular electrophysiology
2. Coding a pipeline with SpikeInterface
3. Downloading data for the course
:::

## Introduction to extracellular electrophysiology {.ephys-process-slide}

::: {.ephys-process-figures}
<div class="ephys-process-left-figure">
  <img src="images/apollo-implant-f4-edited.png" class="ephys-process-image fragment fade-out" data-fragment-index="2" alt="Implant recording, target area, and example extracellular traces across days">
  <img src="images/apollo-implant-f4-edited-w-spikes.png" class="ephys-process-image fragment" data-fragment-index="2" alt="Implant recording, target area, and extracellular traces with spikes highlighted across days">
</div>

<div class="ephys-tuning-figure">
  <img src="images/tuning-curve-image-edited.png" class="ephys-tuning-image fragment" data-fragment-index="4" alt="Orientation tuning curve and preferred orientation schematic">
</div>
:::

::: {.ephys-process-grid}
<div class="ephys-step"><strong>1.</strong> Implant recording electrode</div>
<div class="ephys-step"><span class="fragment" data-fragment-index="1"><strong>2.</strong> Record during behaviour of interest</span></div>
<div class="ephys-step"><span class="fragment" data-fragment-index="2"><strong>3.</strong> Identify action potentials in the recorded trace</span></div>
<div class="ephys-step"><span class="fragment" data-fragment-index="3"><strong>4.</strong> Assign action potentials to neurons with spike sorting</span></div>
<div class="ephys-step"><span class="fragment" data-fragment-index="4"><strong>5.</strong> Understand how neural activity encodes behaviour</span></div>
:::

## Pros and cons {.ephys-compare-slide}

:::: {.ephys-compare-grid}

::: {.ephys-compare-col}
![](images/ephys-positives-examples.png){.ephys-compare-image fig-alt="Targeted deep electrophysiology recording and traces"}

<h3 class="ephys-compare-title">Electrophysiology</h3>

- ✅ High temporal resolution
- ✅ Targetted to deep and multiple regions
- ❌ Cannot easily identify individual neurons
:::

::: {.ephys-compare-col}
![](images/2p-examples.png){.ephys-compare-image fig-alt="Two-photon imaging field of view with labelled neurons"}

<h3 class="ephys-compare-title">Imaging</h3>

- ✅ Easy identification of individual neurons (genetically targetable)
- ❌ Low temporal resolution
- ❌ Limited depth and field of view
:::

::::

## Classic findings

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Orientation selectivity (Hubel and Wiesel, 1962)

<iframe class="video-embed video-embed-classic video-embed-center" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/IOHayh06LJ4" title="Hubel and Wiesel orientation selectivity" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
<div class="fragment">

### Motor population encoding (Georgopoulos, 1986)

![](images/georgeopoulos-1986.png){.classic-findings-image fig-alt="Georgopoulos 1986 motor population encoding figure"}

</div>
:::

::::

## Classic findings

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Hippocampal space encoding

<div class="classic-findings-label classic-findings-label-first">Place cells</div>

<iframe class="video-embed video-embed-place-cell" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/STyd1qJr3yM" title="Hippocampal place cells" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

<div class="classic-findings-label">Theta phase precession</div>

![](images/pheta-phase-precession.png){.classic-findings-image-medium .classic-findings-image-compact fig-alt="Theta phase precession example"}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
<div class="fragment">

### Brain-wide map (The International Brain Laboratory et al., 2023)

![](images/brain-wide-map-ibl.png){.classic-findings-image fig-alt="International Brain Laboratory brain-wide map"}


</div>
:::

::::

## In the clinic

<iframe class="video-embed video-embed-clinic video-embed-center" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/iTZ2N-HJbwA" title="Recording example" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Evolution of recording devices

![](images/evolution-of-recording-devices.png){.slide-image-centered fig-alt="Timeline of recording devices"}

## The recording set up

![](images/the-recording-setup.png){fig-alt="Electrode to amplifier to analog to digital conversion to computer recording setup"}

## Extracellular electrophysiological data {data-transition="none"}

![](images/extrcellular-line-image.png){fig-alt="Extracellular electrophysiology line traces across channels"}

## Extracellular electrophysiological data {data-transition="none"}

![](images/extrcellular-map-image.png){fig-alt="Extracellular electrophysiology spatial map view"}

## The sorting pipeline


## Course timeline review

## SpikeInterface: a common API for open-source electrophysiological tools

![](images/spikeinterface-common-api.png){fig-alt="SpikeInterface common API for open-source electrophysiological tools"}

## The goal of the course: fun and learning!

::: {.r-fit-text style="color:#2e8b57;"}
Mathematical topics and being "comfortable in confusion"
:::

<br>

> It is like being lost in a jungle and trying to use all the knowledge that you can gather to come up with some new tricks, and with some luck, you might find a way out.
>
> — **Maryam Mirzakhani**, Fields medal winner

> So each of these breakthroughs... couldn't exist without the many months of stumbling around in the dark that precede them.
>
> — **Andrew Wiles**, solved Fermat's last theorem

## The goal of the course: fun and learning! {.math-goal-slide}

<div class="math-goal-grid">
  <div class="math-goal-quote">
    <p>It is like being lost in a jungle and trying to use all the knowledge that you can gather to come up with some new tricks, and with some luck, you might find a way out.</p>
    <p><strong>Maryam Mirzakhani</strong>, Fields medal winner</p>
  </div>
  <div class="math-goal-quote">
    <p>So each of these breakthroughs, they are the culmination of, and couldn't exist without, the many months of stumbling around in the dark that precede them.</p>
    <p><strong>Andrew Wiles</strong>, solved Fermat's last theorem</p>
  </div>
  <div class="math-goal-center">Mathematical topics and being "comfortable in confusion"</div>
  <div class="math-goal-quote math-goal-quote-lower">
    <p>My advisor once told me that doing math is 98% being confused and 2% figuring it out.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="math-goal-quote math-goal-quote-lower">
    <p>If I spend an entire day and all I do is understand this one feature of this one object that I didn't understand before, then that's a great day.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="math-goal-author"><strong>Mathematics PhD Students</strong></div>
</div>

## The coding segment

::: {.r-fit-text}
(explain how it will work)

Suggestions on how to run the code
:::
